Title: Azam to sue Albert Tei over allegations of intimidation
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Former Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission chief commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki will proceed with legal action against businessman Albert Tei over allegations made against him.
He said he had instructed his lawyers to serve a legal notice on Tei as soon as possible.
"My lawyers will serve the notice as soon as possible," he said when contacted today.
The development comes after Azam confirmed that he had attended Bukit Aman earlier today to have his
statement recorded
in connection with allegations made by Tei.
Previously, Azam had said he was
considering launching a legal suit
against Tei for slander.
On May 27, CID director Datuk M. Kumar said police had established contact with Azam and would record his statement as part of their investigation.
Azam had previously welcomed the investigation and said he would extend his cooperation to the police.
The case stems from allegations made by Tei, who claimed that Azam had threatened, obstructed and victimised him when he attempted to expose an alleged corruption scandal involving several high-profile politicians in Sabah last year.
Police are investigating the matter under Section 506 of the Penal Code for criminal intimidation.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Watchdog group demands maximum penalty after 'street race' that killed five
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A crime watchdog has called for the authorities to pursue the maximum penalties available under the law against those responsible for a street racing incident that claimed five lives, including four members of a family.
Malaysians Against Rape, Assault and Snatch Thief (Marah) founder Dave Avran described the crash as a preventable tragedy caused by reckless and unlawful behaviour on a public road.
"These victims were ordinary people going about their lives. They did not choose to participate in a race. They did not consent to being placed in danger. Yet they paid the ultimate price for someone else's reckless decision," he told the
New Straits Times
.
He said street racing should not be viewed as entertainment or a harmless thrill, but as a dangerous act capable of destroying lives within seconds.
He said the incident also claimed the life of a 22-year-old man, one of the two allegedly involved in the race, highlighting how reckless behaviour could devastate not only innocent road users but also the families of those responsible.
"No level of education, wealth, family influence or social status should exempt anyone from accountability. When actions result in death and devastation on this scale, the law must be applied in full force, fairly and without favour," he said.
Avran said the victims' families would bear the pain of their loss for the rest of their lives, with parents losing children, children losing parents and families losing loved ones who could never be replaced.
"I urge the authorities to conduct a thorough investigation and seek the maximum penalties available.
"Justice must be based on the rule of law and applied equally to all," he said.
The organisation also called on young drivers and motorcyclists to stop engaging in illegal street racing and dangerous speeding, warning that a few moments of excitement could result in imprisonment, lifelong regret and the loss of innocent lives.
"We must return to a culture where dangerous driving is condemned, accountability is expected and human life is valued above ego, thrill-seeking and reckless behaviour," Avran said.
It was reported that the Attorney-General's Chambers (A-GC) has directed police to classify investigations into the crash that claimed five lives, including a family of four, in Simpang Renggam as
murder and reckless driving causing death
.
Attorney-General Tan Sri Mohd Dusuki Mokhtar, however, had said the final determination on the appropriate charges would only be made after the A-GC had thoroughly reviewed the findings of the police investigation.
"We have instructed that the investigation be classified as murder and reckless driving causing the death of road users," he told the NST.
Dusuki said the A-GC deeply regretted the tragedy and was disappointed that despite stern action taken in previous fatal road accident cases, which included murder charges, there is little deterrent among errant drivers.
The crash, which occurred about 1pm on Monday at Km27 Jalan Renggam-Simpang, involved five vehicles.
Initial police investigations showed that two brothers aged 19 and 22 were driving recklessly at high speed when one of their vehicles, a BMW 530e, went out of control and crashed into a Proton Wira, a Toyota Vios and a Perodua Alza.
All four occupants of the Toyota Vios were killed on the spot while the driver of the BMW succumbed to his injuries while being sent to the hospital.
The victims in the Toyota Vios were identified as Mohd Aiman Mohd Rashid, 36; his wife Nor Azlina Abd Latif, 33; her mother Semek Mat Soh, 73; and his niece Nur Airish Syifa Sidek, 10, who were on their way to Kota Tinggi from Melaka.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Perlis must innovate to tackle economic challenges, says Ruler
Article:
KANGAR: The Perlis state government must adopt innovative approaches tailored to local strengths to address increasingly complex economic challenges, says the Raja of Perlis, Tuanku Syed Sirajuddin Jamalullail.
His Royal Highness said this included strengthening high-value sectors, attracting strategic investments, and leveraging the state's geographical advantages and existing resources.
He said the approaches should be based on best practices proven effective locally and internationally, while taking into account Perlis' specific context.
"Focus should be given to high-impact initiatives capable of generating long-term returns, rather than short-term programmes that offer limited value to the state's economy," he said in his royal address at the opening of the fourth term of the Perlis State Legislative Assembly sitting at Kompleks Seri Putra here today.
Also present were the Raja Perempuan of Perlis, Tuanku Tengku Fauziah Tengku Abdul Rashid; the Raja Muda of Perlis, Tuanku Syed Faizuddin Putra Jamalullail; and the Raja Puan Muda of Perlis, Tuanku Dr Hajah Lailatul Shahreen Akashah Khalil.
Tuanku Syed Sirajuddin also stressed the importance of increasing state revenue and exploring new, more sustainable sources of income.
He said all expenditure must be carried out prudently, transparently and with integrity, while ensuring maximum returns from development projects.
"True progress is not measured solely by figures, but by the level of well-being experienced by all segments of society. Every policy and initiative must therefore be carefully designed to improve the people's quality of life in a comprehensive, inclusive and sustainable manner," he said.
The Perlis Ruler also expressed hope that the state government would continue to maintain strong ties with the federal government to safeguard the well-being of the state.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Six charged in RM192,000 mule account syndicate cases
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Six people, including a woman, were charged at the Sessions Court here today with offences related to mule accounts linked to non-existent loan, investment and job syndicates involving losses totalling RM192,084.
Muhammad Aiman Firdaus Sezaini, 21; Fadzil Abd Aziz, 54; Eric Yeremia Kong, 27; and Sarawut Chaorai, 34, pleaded guilty after the charges were read before Judge Mohd Zamir Suhaimee and Datuk Che Wan Zaidi Che Wan Ibrahim.
Aiman, Fadzil and Sarawut were fined RM1,000 each, while Kong was fined RM450. All four face a month's imprisonment if they fail to pay the fines.
They paid the fines.
According to the charge sheets, the four dishonestly assisted in concealing properties belonging to victims, involving amounts between RM384 and RM2,500.
The offences were committed at several locations in Seri Alam, Johor Jaya and Ulu Tiram between June 18 and Oct 19, 2025.
They were charged under Section 424 of the Penal Code for fraud, which carries a maximum five-year jail term, a fine or both upon conviction.
Meanwhile, Noor Shafiqah Zulaikha Abdul Halim, 22, and Yap Kay Soon, 50, pleaded not guilty to using corporate bank accounts for transactions totalling RM157,000 and RM30,000 without lawful purpose and misappropriating the funds for personal use.
They allegedly committed the offences in Ulu Tiram between Dec 18, 2023 and June 29, 2025. They were charged under Section 424C(1) for using their bank accounts to receive payments and Section 403 of the Penal Code for criminal breach of trust, respectively.
Noor Shafiqah faces a maximum 10-year jail term, a fine not exceeding RM150,000 or both, while Yap faces up to five years' jail, a fine or both if convicted.
Deputy public prosecutors R. Nevina and Nurul Syafiqah Sha'ari prosecuted, while all the accused were unrepresented.
The court allowed bail at RM5,000 for Noor Shafiqah and RM10,000 for Yap with one guarantor each, and fixed July 2 for case mention.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Fatal Serian crash: Car overturns while overtaking tractor
Article:
KUCHING: A failed attempt to overtake a tractor claimed the lives of a family of five, including an infant, after their car veered off the road, overturned and landed in a water-filled drain in Serian today.
Sarawak Police Commissioner Datuk Mohamad Zainal Abdullah said preliminary investigations revealed that the accident occurred at about 8.10am along Jalan Engsengei in Kampung Lubok Ria, when the vehicle was travelling towards Serian.
"The driver is believed to have lost control while overtaking a tractor travelling in the same direction, causing the car to veer off the road, crash into an iron water pipe on the roadside and overturn into a large water-filled drain.
"All five occupants, comprising the driver and four family members, were pronounced
dead
at the scene by paramedics from Serian Hospital," he said in a statement today.
He said the case is being investigated under Section 41(1) of the Road Transport Act 1987 for causing death by reckless or dangerous driving.
Zainal urged motorists to obey traffic regulations and exercise caution, particularly when overtaking and driving in areas with limited visibility during the early morning hours.
The driver was identified as Mohammad Bistari Aidit, 30, while the four passengers were Nur Farah Shahirah Narudin, 26; Ajibah Ahmat, 47; Nur Ain Nasrin Narudin, 16; and two-year-old Nur As-syifa Lathfya Narudin.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Taiwan says Japan, Philippines must respect its rights in maritime border talks
Article:
TAIPEI: Taiwan has asked that Japan and the Philippines respect its rights and territory when they hold talks on their maritime border, Taipei's foreign ministry said on Wednesday.
Japan and the Philippines said last month they would begin formal talks on delimiting the maritime boundary of the exclusive economic zone and the continental shelf between the two countries "in accordance with international law."
China, which views democratically governed Taiwan as its own territory, has condemned the move and this week sent coast guard ships off Taiwan's east coast in response, drawing an angry reaction from Taipei.
Taiwan's foreign ministry said its representative offices in Japan and the Philippines would seek confirmation that the negotiation process and outcomes of any future agreements "will not affect the sovereign rights Taiwan enjoys under international law."
Taiwan's sovereignty over its territory and relevant waters, as well as its rights under international law, are "indisputable", the ministry said.
Given that the maritime area where Japan and the Philippines intend to negotiate boundaries overlaps with Taiwan's exclusive economic zone off its eastern coast, the ministry said it calls on both countries to fully consider Taiwan's rights and interests and engage in talks.
Asked about Taiwan's concerns, Japanese Chief Cabinet Secretary Minoru Kihara said earlier on Wednesday that if Japan and the Philippines agree on the maritime boundaries it "would not be legally binding on third parties."
The Philippines' foreign ministry did not immediately respond to a request for comment.
Taiwan's foreign ministry added that China had no right to claim to speak on its behalf over the maritime boundary issue.
"Taiwan will not tolerate China's attempts to undermine regional peace and stability through so-called coast guard law enforcement and military power projection," the ministry said.
Taiwan's government rejects Beijing's sovereignty claims.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Perlis receives over RM660mil under 13th Malaysia Plan – Perlis Ruler
Article:
KANGAR: Perlis has received RM661.70 million under Rolling Plan 1 (RP1) of the 13th Malaysia Plan (13MP), with expenditure reaching RM87.46 million, or 13.22 per cent, as of April.
The Raja of Perlis, Tuanku Syed Sirajuddin Jamalullail, said the allocation covered 354 projects.
"Under Rolling Plan 5 (RP5) of the 12th Malaysia Plan (12MP), Perlis received RM632.56 million involving 188 projects. Of that, RM474.17 million was spent, or 74.96 per cent last year," he said.
His Royal Highness said this in his royal address at the opening of the fourth term of the Perlis State Legislative Assembly sitting at Kompleks Seri Putra here today. The address was delivered on his behalf by the Raja Muda of Perlis, Tuanku Syed Faizuddin Putra Jamalullail.
Also present were the Raja Perempuan of Perlis, Tuanku Tengku Fauziah Tengku Abdul Rashid, and the Raja Puan Muda of Perlis, Tuanku Dr Hajah Lailatul Shahreen Akashah Khalil.
Tuanku Syed Sirajuddin said all development projects must be expedited so that their benefits reach the people within a reasonable timeframe.
"I also call on all departments and agencies allocated funds to take immediate steps to implement projects and ensure spending is carried out efficiently and in an orderly manner," he said.
He added that efficient implementation was crucial to stimulate economic activity in the state, creating jobs, income opportunities and supporting local businesses.
His Royal Highness said efficiency would also strengthen public confidence and ensure state resources were used optimally without wastage or delay.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Ukrainian haiku poet finds small miracles in war
Article:
A temperamental lift leads to the apartment in central Ukraine of a 27-year-old poet celebrated in Japan but almost unknown in her own country.
With pink hair, fuchsia sweater and matching socks, Vladislava Simonova tells the story of her burgeoning career 7,800 kilometres (4,850 miles) away in a country she has never visited.
But here in the central Ukrainian city of Poltava, she lives near a trolleybus depot, which is just one of the sites targeted by Russian drones whose constant buzzing puts her on edge.
Just as she mentions the word "explosion" to describe the terror of Russian strikes, a drone whizzes overhead and explodes in the distance.
Next to her, a shelf holds 15 books with colourful spines – a collection of contemporary Ukrainian poets – two Japanese teapots, three religious icons and a figurine of Phoebe Buffay from the series "Friends."
"I never thought that I would be writing about war," she told AFP.
"With time, I somehow came to realise that ... tiny details can convey the tragedy of this great war much better than perhaps dozens of reports," she added.
Simonova is among a whole generation of artists bearing witness to the  invasion that has devastated Ukrainian cultural life.
Simonova said she discovered haiku – her preferred form – in 2013, when she was a teenager.
The three-line poems, made up of 17 syllables in a 5-7-5 pattern, were codified in 17th-century Japan to capture the beauty of nature, daily life and fleeting moments with simplicity.
For years, she studied the Japanese masters – Basho, Buson, Issa – and wrote more than 600 haiku which, she said, gradually became less "clumsy":
He walks so proudly,
On soft apricot petals
This plump little cat.
24.04.2015
Not bothered by rain,
I tremble my way back home
With a pine sapling.
16.10.2014
In 2018, Simonova won a competition organised by a Japanese foundation.
When Russia launched its full-scale invasion in February 2022, she was living in Kharkiv.
Russian forces tried to seize the northeastern city and have been shelling it constantly since being pushed back.
For three months when Russian troops first crossed the border, she survived by living in an underground shelter.
Instead of a storm –
The rumbling of explosions.
Springtime has arrived.
14.05.2022
A house in ruins.
Through the hole in the rooftop,
Stars are glimmering.
14.05.2022
In March 2022, from her shelter, Simonova gave a written interview to Japan's The Asahi Shimbun newspaper.
A few weeks later, renowned poet Madoka Mayuzumi got in touch. She told AFP that Simonova has a "deep understanding" of the essence of haiku.
"Even in the midst of war, she gazes up at the moon and stars and admires flowers... her haiku reflect a communion with nature," Mayuzumi said.
"Despite the themes that tend to be sombre, her work possesses a sense of optimism," Mayuzumi added.
[[nid:1454529]]
Bees oblivious
To the air-raid siren's sound.
Linden trees in bloom.
19.06.2022
With around 10 others, Mayuzumi helped Simonova translate and publish her first collection in Japan in 2023.
The book received "very high praise", Mayuzumi said.
Throughout Japan's history, she added, people have written haiku in dark times, including after the 1945 atomic bombings and the 2011 tsunami.
In August 2022, the underground shelter in Kharkiv where Simonova had lived was destroyed by a Russian missile. She moved to Poltava.
She published a second collection in Japan in 2024, followed by another in Denmark in early 2026.
She dreams of publishing one in Ukraine.
Before the war, she wrote in Russian. She later switched to Ukrainian.
The translation of the poems was complex. The two related languages often use words of different lengths – "umbrella", for example, is one syllable in Russian, but four in Ukrainian.
Simonova does not read prose, "only poetry." And the Bible. She belongs to Poltava's tiny Catholic community.
During AFP's visit, she suggests going to the park, says goodbye to her husband – who stays at home – before hurrying down the stairs of her Soviet-era apartment block. The lift was not working.
It is a cold spring Sunday and the park is almost empty. She sits on a tree branch near a pond, wearing a multicoloured puffer jacket.
Since childhood, Simonova has suffered from a serious heart condition that leaves her exhausted.
She discovered haiku in a hospital, in an anthology that also contained "Persian poems."
As the wind blows, she stands up and reads aloud for the first time in public, reciting each poem twice.
The first is for friends no longer around:
They scatter away
Like cherry blossoms in wind,
People I hold close.
The second is a memory of Kharkiv.
I clutch in my palm
Some fragments of a missile.
The pain stays with me.
She leafs through her pink-covered collection, then chooses one last poem.
What a sky it is!
And yet from that very sky
Missiles fall on us.
* The writer is from AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 95pct of area searched; search for Jaslinda to be scaled down
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Search and rescue (SAR) personnel have covered about 95 per cent of the designated search area in their efforts to locate missing hiker Jaslinda Saludin at Gunung Batu Putih, Tapah.
District police chief Superintendent Abdul Malik Hasim said the extensive search effort, involving 116 personnel from multiple agencies over the past 11 days, was among the factors behind the decision to scale down the operation beginning tomorrow.
"We have searched approximately 95 per cent of the area using various methods employed by the Fire and Rescue Department and other agencies involved in the operation," he told reporters today.
Abdul Malik said the decision was reached during a SAR coordination meeting chaired by the Batang Padang district officer earlier today.
MORE TO COME
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia's gas supply stable, no immediate shortage risk: MOSTI, Gas Malaysia
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's gas supply remains stable with no immediate risk of shortages despite ongoing uncertainty in global energy markets.
Science, Technology and Innovation Minister Chang Lih Kang said the country's supply position remains sufficient, with electricity generation largely supported by domestic resources.
He noted that about 80 per cent of Malaysia's electricity generation is derived from domestic sources, while the remaining 20 per cent relies on imports, helping to limit exposure to external disruptions.
"As far as I know, the supply is okay. "We still have enough supply, and there is no need to worry too much," he told reporters after launching the Revolutionising Industry with Graphene initiative and witnessing the signing of Asean's first LOOP technology deployment between Gas Malaysia Bhd and Levidian here today.
Gas Malaysia president and group chief executive officer Azli Mohamed said there is currently no shortage of gas supply, with volumes received from Petroliam Nasional Bhd (Petronas) sufficient to meet demand.
"For now, there is no shortage in the industry. The supply we get from Petronas is enough," he said.
Azli said Gas Malaysia's customers are not facing any issues with gas usage, noting that Malaysia's gas supply is sourced mainly from domestic production and Australia, with limited dependence on the Middle East.
"In terms of industry needs, there is no problem. We can supply more than 1,000 of our customers," he said.
While gas supply remains adequate, Azli said broader challenges facing industries relate more to transportation costs and supply chain disruptions.
He noted that the glove manufacturing sector, one of Gas Malaysia's largest customer segments, depends heavily on raw materials sourced from South Korea and China, making supply chain resilience an important consideration.
"The issue is usually supply chain, getting workers, but the impact of the energy crisis has not yet hit them," he said.
Looking ahead, Azli said it was still too early to predict conditions over the next two to three years, with the industry currently focused on the next six to 12 months.
"I think that will be a correction period," he said.
He added that Gas Malaysia maintains regular engagement with customers to better understand their operational challenges and business dependencies beyond energy costs, particularly as many of the industries it serves play a significant role in the national economy.
On supply security, Azli said Gas Malaysia's sole supplier is Petronas and that the company operates under a back-to-back arrangement that helps ensure supply stability.
Asked whether global conflicts could affect gas prices, he said gas prices generally have some correlation with international energy benchmarks but stressed that the company remains focused on domestic supply conditions.
"As long as the volume of gas and our back-to-back arrangement with Petronas, that will not be an issue," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Batu Kawan Industrial Park issues settled following PDC, Aspen meeting
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: Penang Development Corporation (PDC) and Aspen (Group) Holdings Ltd have agreed to consider all matters relating to the Batu Kawan Industrial Park 2 (BKIP2) request for proposal (RFP) exercise formally closed.
This follows a meeting between both parties to resolve outstanding issues.
In a joint statement today, both parties said they had met to discuss concerns that had entered the public domain regarding the BKIP2 RFP process and had reached a mutual understanding on all outstanding matters.
PDC said, as the evaluating authority, it had exercised its full discretion and prerogative in assessing all proposals submitted under the RFP exercise.
The agency said its decision was made in accordance with its mandate to act in the best interests of Penang and its people.
Aspen acknowledged and respected PDC's authority as the evaluating body and said it did not challenge the outcome of the RFP exercise.
The statement also addressed public clarifications issued by Aspen on May 22 and PDC's subsequent response on May 28.
Both parties said their respective public statements were made in good faith based on their understanding of the proposal and evaluation process at the time.
On the issue of bridge construction, PDC clarified that its assessment of Aspen's consortium proposal noted that a request for a first right of refusal (FROR) over approximately 580 acres of adjacent PDC land had been presented in proximity to the consortium's bridge construction commitment.
PDC said it had interpreted the two elements as operationally linked and had formed the view that the bridge construction commitment was conditional upon the granting of the FROR.
However, both parties said they had since met and discussed the matter directly and now considered the issue resolved.
They also agreed that direct engagement and dialogue between evaluating authorities and bidders in major RFP exercises would help resolve differences in interpretation more efficiently and in the interests of all parties.
"Neither party will make further public statements regarding the BKIP2 RFP exercise," they said.
"We also reaffirmed our commitment to Penang's continued growth and development and expressed their intention to contribute positively to the state's future," they added.
Chief Minister and PDC chairman Chow Kon Yeow was recently engaged in a war of words with his predecessor Lim Guan Eng at the state assembly sitting over the rejection of an RM818 million bid by an IJM-Aspen-Mettiz Capital consortium to develop 559 acres in Ladang Byram/Changkat, citing a potential RM294 million cost exposure for the corporation.
Chow had said that the bid, submitted under a 2024 RFP was rejected because it
violated original tender conditions
and imposed pre-conditions on PDC.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Perlis Ruler wants MB be given space to govern without interference
Article:
KANGAR: The Raja of Perlis, Tuanku Syed Sirajuddin Jamalullail, has called for Menteri Besar Abu Bakar Hamzah to be given space to lead the state administration until the end of the current state assembly term without interference.
His Royal Highness said political stability was crucial in boosting investor confidence in the state.
He urged all state assemblymen to set aside differences and extend full cooperation to the Menteri Besar for the benefit of the people and the state.
"I wish to remind all Honourable Members that this is my government. Therefore, I hope the Menteri Besar will be given the space to continue leading until the end of the 15th Perlis State Legislative Assembly term without interference that could disrupt planned development," he said in his royal address at the opening of the fourth term sitting at Kompleks Seri Putra here today.
The address was delivered on his behalf by the Raja Muda of Perlis, Tuanku Syed Faizuddin Putra Jamalullail.
Tuanku Syed Sirajuddin said political differences should not become an obstacle to development and investment efforts in the state.
He also stressed that the state government must maintain strong ties with the federal government, particularly in securing development allocations and support for the people of Perlis.
"I commend the series of visits and courtesy calls made by the Menteri Besar to various ministries to strengthen cooperation between the state and federal governments.
"I hope these efforts will continue to be enhanced to ensure the state's development agenda can be implemented smoothly and effectively," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Azam Baki quizzed over Bloomberg article, Albert Tei claims
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Police today recorded a statement from former Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission chief commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki to facilitate two probes.
Bukit Aman Criminal Investigation Department director Datuk M. Kumar said the first investigation involved the publication of an article by Bloomberg.com which allegedly contained defamatory claims over ownership of Velocity Capital Partner Bhd shares.
"The second case involves allegations of abuse of power and criminal intimidation against a member of the public named Albert Tei," he said in a statement today.
MORE TO COME
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Convenience store opened only five days before robbery
Article:
NIBONG TEBAL: The convenience store in Sungai Daun here, that was robbed by armed assailants, had been operating for only five days before the incident, neighbouring traders said.
The traders, who spoke to the New Straits Times on condition of anonymity, said they had not had the chance to know the store operator personally yet.
They also claimed that this was not the first time a robbery had occurred in their neighbourhood.
A workshop operator, in his 50s, claimed equipment and materials were stolen from his lorry yesterday. He had lodged a police report.
"Wires and other items were taken. I am not sure if it's the same robbers.
"Many shop owners here have installed CCTV cameras because of such cases," he said.
Checks by the New Straits Times showed that about 10 shops are operating in the neighbourhood.
The convenience store that was robbed had been closed for operations since the incident on Sunday night. Only the convenience store operated at night.
The workshop operator described the area as relatively quiet at night, adding that many traders now feared becoming the next target.
Another convenience store operator, in her 50s, said the store that was robbed was very new with not much stocks in the premises.
She said the incident had left traders worried about their safety, especially those who worked alone.
She recounted how her own premises had previously been targeted by thieves who stole cash and other items.
Neighbouring businesses had also reported theft cases, contributing to concerns among traders over security in the area.
The workshop operator said police patrols were regularly seen in the area, but business owners hoped for increased surveillance following the latest incident.
They also expressed hope that the perpetrators would be arrested soon and that additional security measures would be introduced to deter further crimes.
The
robbery
, which was captured on CCTV footage, left a 27-year-old man injured after he was reportedly attacked by armed robbers. The victim sustained injuries to his head and both hands.
The suspects fled with cash, several cartons of cigarettes and a sling bag.
Efforts to trace and apprehend the suspect are ongoing.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Mikro MSC, HKCT to explore large-scale battery storage projects in Malaysia
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Mikro MSC Bhd has entered into a memorandum of agreement with Hong Kong Cospower Technology Co Ltd (HKCT) to explore the development of large-scale utility Battery Energy Storage System (BESS) projects in Malaysia.
The partnership is aligned with the country's energy transition agenda under the National Energy Transition Roadmap (NETR), which is expected to unlock investment opportunities worth between RM1.2 trillion and RM1.3 trillion by 2050.
Both Mikro MSC and HKCT will also focus on opportunities under the NETR through the Malaysia Battery Energy Storage System (MyBeST) programme, the country's first competitive open-bidding initiative for grid-scale energy storage projects regulated by the Energy Commission.
Mikro MSC chief executive officer Syed Mohd Hafiz Syed Mohd said the company is well-positioned to capitalise on opportunities created under the NETR.
"We are in a strong position to capture the significant opportunities that NETR presents for the future of the nation's energy sector and long-term sustainable economic growth.
"Through our collaboration with HKCT, we gain a world-class strategic partner in battery energy storage technology that offers advanced and cost-effective solutions aligned with Malaysia's energy transition aspirations," he said in his opening remarks.
The signing ceremony was witnessed by Deputy Prime Minister and Energy Transition and Water Transformation Minister Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof.
Launched in November 2024, the MyBeST programme aims to deliver 400 megawatts (MW) of battery capacity and 1,600 megawatt-hours (MWh) of energy storage capacity through four projects across Peninsular Malaysia.
The initiative supports Malaysia's target of achieving 70 per cent renewable energy capacity by 2050.
The agreement marks Mikro MSC's expansion into the energy storage sector as Malaysia pushes to strengthen grid resilience and sustainability.
Under the two-year agreement, both parties will identify, develop, evaluate, negotiate and implement BESS projects across the country.
HKCT vice president William Wang said Malaysia represents one of the most promising markets for grid-scale energy storage growth in Southeast Asia.
"The decision to appoint Mikro MSC as our exclusive partner in Malaysia was based on the company's technical expertise, strong market position and proven track record in supporting large-scale energy infrastructure projects.
"We look forward to working closely with Mikro MSC to support the development of Malaysia's energy storage sector and establish a long-term presence in this strategic market," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Police urge MHO to share data on alleged Malaysia scam hubs
Article:
SHAH ALAM: Police have urged the Malaysian Humanitarian Organisation (MHO) to share information regarding claims that online scam syndicates previously operating in Myanmar, Laos and Cambodia have relocated their operations to Malaysia.
Bukit Aman Commercial Crime Investigation Department (CCID) director Datuk Rusdi Mohd Isa said the allegations could not be verified at this stage, but assured that police would look into any information provided.
"Police welcome and will obtain further information from MHO for further action.
"The CCID views such allegations seriously and we remain vigilant against the evolving modus operandi of scam syndicates," he said when contacted today.
His remarks came in response to claims by MHO secretary-general Datuk Hishamuddin Hashim that international scam syndicates have begun
using Malaysia as a new operational hub
to avoid detection following large-scale enforcement operations by the Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI).
Hishamuddin said MHO would soon submit information on the alleged scam centres to the authorities.
He claimed the syndicates had adopted a strategy of relocating and operating discreetly from strategic locations in Malaysia for more than six months.
On May 10, the CCID reported an increase in arrests and charges related to commercial crimes during the first three months of this year compared with the corresponding period last year.
A total of 7,190 suspects were arrested between January and March this year, compared with 7,084 during the same period in 2025, representing an increase of 106 arrests or 1.5 per cent.
The number of charges also rose to 5,140 cases during the period, up from 4,525 cases recorded a year earlier.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Former Johor MB Hasni plays down parliamentary move as BN candidate speculation mounts
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Speculation has been swirling around former Johor menteri besar Datuk Seri Hasni Mohammad and whether he will defend his Benut seat.
Party sources suggest the senior Umno leader could forgo defending the state seat and instead be positioned to contest a parliamentary constituency in the next general election.
However, when contacted by the
New Straits Times
, Hasni said discussions with Johor Barisan Nasional chairman and caretaker Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi, had so far indicated no change to the existing arrangement.
"As of my last discussion with Datuk (Onn Hafiz), the status quo remains," he said.
Hasni said that candidate selection was still at an early stage and would undergo several rounds of scrutiny before being finalised.
"The list will go through several processes, perhaps more than three rounds, before a final decision is made.
"At this stage, it is too early to make any predictions," he said.
The former Johor BN chairman added that he would accept whatever decision was ultimately made by Onn Hafiz and the party's central leadership.
On Monday, Onn Hafiz announced the
dissolution of the state assembly
, paving the way for Johor's 16th state election, to be held within 60 days.
The early dissolution of the state assembly, ahead of its April 2027 term expiry, had been widely anticipated following Onn Hafiz's announcement last month that BN would
contest all 56 state seats
on its own.
With the state polls now on the horizon, attention has shifted to BN's candidate line-up.
Johor Umno sources said Onn Hafiz is widely tipped to defend Machap, the seat he won with a 6,543-vote majority in the 2022 state election.
The caretaker menteri besar is also expected to remain BN's choice to lead the state should the coalition secure another mandate.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 'Papa is gone, I'd rather be dead': Son of delivery rider killed in crash haunted by guilt
Article:
KLANG: The death of delivery rider Amirul Hafiz Omar in a collision at Jalan Raya Barat in March has plunged his family into darkness, particularly his three young children.
His widow, Nor Nadia Abdul Majid, 32, said her eldest son, Muhammad Aqif Amsyar, nine, continues to struggle with guilt, believing he was responsible for his father's death.
Amirul, 32, was on his way to buy a mathematics book for him when the fatal accident occurred.
"My son keeps blaming himself. He says he is no longer happy and that life is not the same without his papa.
"He even said he would rather be dead.
"He feels responsible because his father was doing a favour for him when the accident happened.
"But I keep reminding him that it was not his fault and that the driver was responsible for what happened," she said.
She said the family is still trying to come to terms with Amirul's sudden death and the immense void he has left behind.
"We are no longer the happy family we once were," she said through tears.
Nadia said her second son, Muhammad Afif Izz Rayqal, seven, is constantly reminded of Amirul by the belongings he left behind.
"One day, he asked me, 'Do I need to pay to see Papa?'"
Following Amirul's death, she and the boys, including the youngest, Nur Humaira, two, moved to Perak to be closer to family support.
Nadia travelled from Perak to the Klang courts complex today to attend the mention proceedings involving technician R. Saktygaanapathy, 28, who is facing charges of murdering Amirul and self-administering drugs.
Saktygaanapathy was charged
with the murder of Amirul at 11.47am along Jalan Raya Barat on March 29.
No plea was recorded as the case falls under the jurisdiction of the High Court.
The charge, framed under Section 302 of the Penal Code, carries the death penalty or imprisonment of between 30 and 40 years upon conviction.
If the death sentence is not imposed, the offender must be whipped at least 12 times.
He is being held on remand at Sungai Buloh Prison.
Separately, he claimed trial to an amended charge of self-administering tetrahydrocannabinol at 3.30pm at the Klang Selatan district police headquarters narcotics investigation department on March 29.
The charge, framed under Section 15(1)(a) of the Dangerous Drugs Act, carries a maximum RM5,000 fine or a jail term not exceeding two years and police supervision not exceeding three years.
Both cases will be mentioned on July 22.
According to police investigations, Saktygaanapathy allegedly crashed his car head-on into Amirul, who was riding a motorcycle on his way to buy a mathematics book for his child.
Amirul was killed instantly after being struck by the car driven by the accused, who was allegedly travelling at high speed towards Klang town from
Jalan Raya Barat
.
He is believed to have overtaken several vehicles before veering into the opposite lane and colliding head-on with Amirul's motorcycle.
The impact flung Amirul onto a Perodua Myvi. He was pronounced dead at the scene.
He was buried at the Kampung Repoh Islamic Cemetery in Larut, Perak.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Trooping of the Colours reflects armed forces' loyalty to King and nation
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Trooping of the Colours ceremony reflects the armed forces' supreme dedication and unwavering loyalty to the Yang di-Pertuan Agong and the nation, while symbolising respect for military history and tradition.
Commander of the 12th Malaysian Infantry Brigade, Brigadier-General Mahamad Fazli Mat Idris said the Colours represented sovereignty within the armed forces and are regarded as the highest symbols of honour, dignity and unit identity.
He said the ceremony was among the most significant in the military calendar, traditionally held three times during the Yang di-Pertuan Agong's five-year reign – in the first, third and final years.
"These Colours carry a long history and are regarded as the highest symbol to be defended at all times. That is why those entrusted with carrying or guarding them take immense pride in the duty," he said in an interview on Bernama Radio's Fokus 30 programme.
This year's ceremony is being held in conjunction with the third year of the reign of His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, following its first staging in 2024. The next parade is scheduled for 2028, the final year of His Majesty's reign.
The ceremony, to be held at Dataran Pahlawan Negara in Putrajaya on June 5, will feature five Colours of His Majesty the Yang di-Pertuan Agong, carried by a combined contingent from the army, the Royal Malaysian Navy and Royal Malaysian Air Force.
A total of 41 officers and 635 other ranks will take part in the parade, while overall participation, including administrative and support personnel, will involve 148 officers and 1,192 other ranks.
Mahamad Fazli said the Colours, awarded by the Yang di-Pertuan Agong to the armed forces' regiments, corps and services, were not merely marks of distinction but historical records of sacrifice, reflected through battle honours embroidered on them.
He said the Colours also served as a bridge between the public and the armed forces' history, sacrifices and role in defending the nation, in line with a whole-of-government and whole-of-society defence approach.
He also encouraged the public to watch the live broadcast of the ceremony on RTM on June 5 to better appreciate its significance as a symbol of unity, sovereignty, peace and the close bond between the royal institution, the armed forces and the people.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: SAR for missing hiker Jaslinda continues
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The search and rescue (SAR) operation for missing hiker Jaslinda Saludin, 49, at Gunung Batu Putih near Tapah, Perak, will continue, albeit on a smaller scale, police said today.
Tapah district police chief Superintendent Abdul Malik Hasim said authorities remain committed to locating Jaslinda despite the operation entering its later stages on Day 11.
He said the SAR operation currently involves 116 personnel from multiple agencies.
However, moving forward, the search effort will be streamlined and primarily carried out by the Fire and Rescue Department, the Forestry Department, residents and non-governmental organisations (NGOs).
"The search will continue, but on a smaller scale. We are not calling off the operation, and efforts to locate the victim will remain ongoing," he said.
Jaslinda was reported missing during a Trans Spencer Chapman hiking expedition in Tapah.
MORE TO COME
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor PKR grassroots back Prakash for Tiram seat
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Calls are growing among Johor Parti Keadilan Rakyat (PKR) grassroots members for Pakatan Harapan (PH) to field Tebrau PKR chief M. Prakash as its candidate for the Tiram seat in the upcoming state election.
State grassroot supporters urged party leaders to field a candidate with strong community credentials.
For the record, Tiram was won by Barisan Nasional's Azizul Bachok in the 2022 Johor state election with a majority of 5,281 votes in a six-corner fight against PKR, Pas, Pejuang, Parti Bangsa Malaysia and two independent candidates.
In a statement, party members and supporters say the candidate fielded in Tiram should have an established track record of service, strong grassroots acceptance, and close ties with the local community.
Supporters view Prakash as a long-serving grassroots leader with consistent engagement at community level.
Prakash is said to have been active on the ground for years, building networks across different communities and working closely with various segments of society through political and non-governmental platforms.
Beyond his party role, he is also involved in several non-governmental organisations, where he has participated in community outreach programmes spanning welfare, education, and social development initiatives.
Supporters describe him as a leader who has maintained a steady presence in multi-ethnic community programmes involving Malays, Chinese, Indians, as well as communities from Sabah and Sarawak, strengthening his image as a unifying grassroots figure.
PKR grassroots members argue that such experience and cross-community engagement make Prakash a suitable contender for the Tiram seat, who will ensure PKR's continuity in service delivery agendas in the constituency.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Police tracking down suspects in viral Kaamatan brawl in Penampang
Article:
PENAMPANG: Police are tracking down those involved in a brawl believed to have occurred near the Kadazan Dusun Cultural Association (KDCA) grounds during the recent Kaamatan celebrations.
The incident, which went viral on social media, is believed to have stemmed from a misunderstanding between several people.
Penampang deputy police chief Deputy Superintendent Erwansyah Abdul Basir said preliminary findings showed no weapons were used in the fight, which was later broken up by members of the public.
"Several people have been called in to have their statements recorded to assist investigations.
"Police are working to identify all those involved and locate witnesses to the incident," he said in a statement today.
He added that police were reviewing video footage circulating on social media to assist in the investigation.
Earlier, several clips showing a group fighting outside the KDCA grounds went viral online and drew public attention.
The footage showed people punching and grappling before the situation was brought under control by members of the public.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Post-mortem report on lost hiker's death pending laboratory analysis
Article:
PETALING JAYA: The post-mortem findings into the death of Nur Izzati Humaira Azizul, whose body was discovered after she went missing while hiking at Bukit Changkat Asa in Hulu Selangor last week, remain pending laboratory analysis.
Hulu Selangor deputy police chief Deputy Superintendent Mohd Asri Mohd Yunus said police were still awaiting the full reports from the Chemistry Department and medical specialists at Sungai Buloh Hospital.
"So far, the case remains classified as a sudden death report (SDR), and investigations are ongoing," he said when contacted today.
The 19-year-old was
found dead
on May 30, four days after she was reported missing while hiking at Bukit Changkat Asa on May 26.
She is believed to have become separated from her family while descending the hill alone at about 10.50am after deciding to move ahead in an attempt to reach the bottom earlier due to the hot weather.
Following the discovery of her body, police classified the case as a
sudden death
report pending further investigation.
Authorities were alerted to her disappearance after receiving information from the Tanjong Malim Fire and Rescue Station at about 2.30pm on May 26.
A four-day search and rescue operation involving 114 personnel from multiple agencies was launched to locate the teenager.
The operation included police, the Fire and Rescue Department, the Civil Defence Force (CDF), mountain guides, volunteers from Universiti Pendidikan Sultan Idris (UPSI), the Welfare Department, local authorities and other volunteers.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Ex-bouncer gets five years' jail for blinding man in nightclub assault
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A former nightclub bouncer was jailed five years for assaulting a man and leaving him blind in his right eye at a nightclub 10 years ago.
Sessions Court judge Mazuliana Abdul Rashid handed down the sentence against Jamsari Idris, 53, after he was found guilty to the offence at the end of defence case today.
Jamsari was charged with causing grievous hurt to Muhammad Ramdhan Aidil Norsham, 28, at a nightclub in Dang Wangi here at 3.30am on Nov 6, 2016.
The charge was framed under Section 325 of the Penal Code, which provides for a maximum prison term of seven years and a fine.
In her judgment, Mazuliana rejected the defence's argument that the accused had merely calmed the victim, ruling that his testimony was unreasonable.
"During cross-examination, the accused failed to explain how he had calmed the victim and repeatedly answered 'I do not know'.
"He also admitted there was no supporting evidence to rebut the allegation that he assaulted the victim.
"The accused's defence amounted to a bare denial without any reasonable explanation and does not constitute a valid defence in law," she said.
The court ordered Jamsari to begin serving his sentence immediately after rejecting the defence's application for a stay of execution pending an appeal to the High Court.
Earlier, Ramdhan told the court in his victim impact statement that before the incident, he had clear vision and was able to hold a glass without dropping it, ride a motorcycle and drive a car like anyone else.
"However, after the incident, my life changed. I was involved in an accident when the motorcycle I was riding hit a pothole, causing three of my toes to break.
"I also lost my job and applied to 12 companies, but received no response.
"I later started a nasi lemak stall with my wife, earning between RM3,000 and RM4,000 a month, and receive RM400 in monthly assistance from the Welfare Department.
"I need to earn more to support my wife, three children and my parents," he said, while urging the court to impose an appropriate sentence on the accused.
Jamsari was represented by lawyer Shah Rizal Abdul Manan, while deputy public prosecutor Ravindejit Kaur appeared for the prosecution.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Man arrested for raping 16-year-old he met just 3 days earlier
Article:
TAMPIN: A 19-year-old man was arrested for allegedly raping a 16-year-old girl only three days after they had met.
District police chief Superintendent Amiruddien Sariman said police received a report on the incident at 1.51am on May 28.
He said preliminary investigations found that the suspect had picked up the teenager from her home in Seremban on a Modenas Kriss motorcycle about 1am on May 23.
"The suspect is believed to have taken the victim to an area in Tampin, where the alleged rape occurred in a bushy area," he said.
Acting on information received, a team from the Tampin police headquarters' Criminal Investigation Department arrested the suspect at his home in Kuala Pilah about 10.10pm yesterday.
Amiruddien said investigations revealed that the victim and suspect had exchanged phone numbers and had been acquainted for only three days before the incident.
The case is being investigated under Section 376 of the Penal Code for rape, which carries a jail term of up to 20 years, with whipping, upon conviction.
The suspect has been remanded for seven days to assist investigations.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: SRKK AI inks underwriting deal with TA Securities ahead of ACE Market IPO
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Digital transformation and technology solutions provider SRKK AI Bhd has signed an underwriting agreement with TA Securities Holdings Bhd ahead of its initial public offering (IPO) on the Ace Market of Bursa Malaysia.
The IPO entails a public issue of 64 million new shares and an offer for sale of 13 million shares.
Of the new shares, 14.2 million shares will be made available for the Malaysian public, 17.04 million are allocated for eligible persons of the company, and 32.76 million will be made available by private placement to selected investors.
SRKK AI chief executive officer Yew Lip Sin highlighted the company's growth during Malaysia's technology wave over the past 20 years through the Multimedia Super Corridor and ensuring digital transformation initiatives.
The company is slated to list on the Ace Market of Bursa Malaysia in the third quarter of 2026.
The IPO proceeds will position the company to tap into the growing momentum of AI adoption across Southeast Asia as regional enterprises begin to harness AI technologies for greater innovation, competitiveness and modernisation.
SRKK AI implements end-to-end digital transformation solutions for enterprises and embeds artificial intelligence (AI) into enterprises' information technology (IT) lifecycles, across cybersecurity, business workflows, cloud, software and analytics.
It is one of the managed partners of Microsoft and the first Malaysian solution provider to earn all pillars of key technical competency under the Microsoft AI Cloud Partner programme.
TA Securities is the principal adviser, sponsor, underwriter and placement agent for SRKK AI's IPO exercise.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Negri Sembilan risks constitutional crisis if assembly misses June 10 deadline
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Negri Sembilan could face a constitutional crisis by June 10 if the State Legislative Assembly does not convene before that date, according to a former Barisan Nasional (BN) strategist.
Former BN strategic communications deputy director Datuk Eric See-To said the Negri Sembilan Constitution requires the state assembly to meet within six months of its previous sitting, which was held on Dec 11, 2025.
He claimed that if the assembly fails to convene within the six-month period stipulated under the state constitution, questions could arise over the possibility of an automatic dissolution.
"Under Article LVI(1), no more than six months may elapse between the last sitting in one session and the date appointed for the first sitting in the next session.
"This means that if an actual state assembly sitting is not held by June 10 at the latest, Negri Sembilan could face a very serious constitutional issue," he said in a Facebook post.
On April 23, the opening of the 15th Negri Sembilan State Legislative Assembly was officiated by the Yang di-Pertuan Besar of Negri Sembilan, Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir, before being adjourned to a date to be announced later.
The opening ceremony was not attended by the Undang Yang Empat and the Tunku Besar of Tampin, as is customary, following a crisis involving claims that the Undang Luak had exercised their powers to remove Tuanku Muhriz as Yang di-Pertuan Besar, while the Undang of Sungai Ujong was reportedly dismissed.
Eric argued that what took place on April 23 was merely the formal opening of the assembly and the delivery of the Royal Address, while the actual business of the assembly, including question-and-answer sessions and debates, did not take place.
No Bills were tabled or passed during the sitting.
"So, if the Negri Sembilan government wants to regard April 23 as an actual state assembly sitting merely to claim it has not breached the state constitution, that is nothing to be proud of.
"The key word in the constitution is 'sitting', not merely an opening ceremony, not simply attending, listening to a speech and then postponing all substantive assembly business to an unknown date," he claimed.
Meanwhile, constitutional expert Professor Dr Nik Ahmad Kamal Nik Mahmod said that if the assembly does not sit within six months, it would amount to a breach of the state constitution.
However, he said whether such a situation would trigger an automatic dissolution remained open to debate, as there is no clear provision governing the matter.
"The suggestion that an automatic dissolution could occur is purely speculative because the provision is not clear.
"There is no court decision on the matter and the state constitution does not expressly provide for it. Therefore, different interpretations may be advanced," he said.
The status of the Negri Sembilan State Legislative Assembly has come under renewed scrutiny amid questions over whether Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun will announce the dissolution of the assembly in the near future or continue its term for the time being, while the state grapples with constitutional and customary institutional issues.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Man jailed three months for outraging sister-in-law's modesty
Article:
BATU PAHAT: A former factory worker was sentenced to three months' jail by the magistrate's court here today after pleading guilty to using criminal force to molest his sister-in-law last month.
Magistrate Arun Noval Dass meted out the sentence after the 26-year-old married accused pleaded guilty to the charge.
According to the charge sheet, the accused used criminal force by pulling the hands of his 23-year-old sister-in-law with the intent to outrage her modesty at his father-in-law's house in the district at 8.45am on May 26.
The offence was framed under Section 354 of the Penal Code, which carries a maximum penalty of 10 years' imprisonment, a fine, whipping, or any two of the punishments.
Based on the facts of the case, the victim lodged a police report stating that the accused entered her room, pulled her hands, and forced her to lie down.
Prior to the incident, the victim was showering when she noticed the presence of the accused.
She then got dressed and went into her room to retrieve her belongings.
When the accused attacked her, the victim scolded him.
Terrified, she fled the room and locked herself inside an adjacent bedroom to protect herself.
Acting on the report, police arrested the accused at 5.45pm the following day.
Deputy public prosecutor Firdaus Ruslan prosecuted, while the accused was represented by lawyer Mohd Razak Sharif.
Earlier, Razak pleaded for a minimum sentence, stating that his client deeply regretted his actions.
"The defence requests consideration for a short prison term to serve as a lesson," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Ford to recall nearly 420,000 US vehicles over seat-belt issue
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Ford Motor will recall 419,967 vehicles in the US as seat belts that do not retract or extend properly can fail to restrain an occupant as intended, increasing the risk of injury in a crash, the US National Highway Traffic Safety Administration said on Wednesday.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: OECD cuts 2026 global growth forecasts over Mideast war fallout
Article:
PARIS: The war in the Middle East has dented economic growth prospects worldwide, with a more severe shock likely if no effective ceasefire is agreed before 2027, the OECD warned Wednesday.
Global economic growth is now forecast to reach 2.8 percent this year if Gulf exports of oil and gas return to pre-conflict levels in the third quarter, the group of 38 industrialised countries said in its quarterly update.
If the Mideast war continues into next year, however, global growth could slow to 2.1 per cent, the OECD said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Another PKR lawmaker claims access blocked to constituency fund portal
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 3 — The Petaling Jaya parliamentary office has been unable to access the MyKhas system since May 29, 2026, its MP Lee Chean Chung said in a statement today, making him the second lawmaker allegedly barred from the portal that provides access to constituency development funds.
Lee said he issued the public statement to seek clarification from Putrajaya, stressing that critical matters related to constituency allocations and Projek Mesra Rakyat (PMR) have been disrupted.
“To ensure that the rakyat (people) are not left wondering and waiting in uncertainty, I have decided to disclose this matter publicly today,” Lee wrote.
“In our country’s political history, we have witnessed several opposition MPs, including six from Parti Pribumi Bersatu Malaysia (Bersatu), receiving constituency allocations after declaring their support for the Unity Government.
“Yet, to the best of my knowledge, this might be the first time in the nation’s history where two government MPs have lost access to their constituency allocation channels,” the PKR leader added.
MyKhas is operated by the Implementation Coordination Unit (ICU) under the Prime Minister’s Department (PMD).
Both Lee and Subang MP Wong Chen are known allies of former minister and PKR defector Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli.
Rafizi, together with Nik Nazmi Nik Ahmad, also a former minister, has formed a new party set to challenge Pakatan Harapan in seats previously contested by PKR in the upcoming elections.
Wong had speculated that he was likely barred from accessing his constituency funds after attending the launch of Parti Bersama Malaysia. Lee suggested the same, but said PKR leadership should at least provide a clear reason.
“If this decision is related to my attendance at the launch event of the new party led by Rafizi Ramli and Nik Nazmi Nik Ahmad on May 17, then the party leadership should provide a clear and open explanation,” the MP wrote.
“If my actions are deemed a breach of party discipline, disloyal to the party, or detrimental to the party’s interests, then due process must be carried out. A warning letter could be issued, a show-cause letter could be requested, or a disciplinary investigation could even be conducted through the established channels.”
On May 22, PKR secretary-general Datuk Fuziah Salleh reportedly said the party leadership would contact the six MPs who attended the event, including Lee.
The PJ lawmaker said he has yet to receive any official notification.
“In any political organisation, the principles of accountability and due process should be respected,” Lee wrote.
“What has happened this time is deeply disappointing because no verbal or written explanation was provided before access to the constituency allocation was blocked.”

Title: Police record Azam Baki’s statement in two separate probes
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 3 — Police have recorded a statement from Tan Sri Azam Baki in connection with two separate investigations involving allegations of defamation, abuse of power, and criminal intimidation.
Bukit Aman Criminal Investigation Department director Datuk M. Kumar said the statement was recorded under Section 112 of the Criminal Procedure Code.
“The first case concerns the publication of an article by Bloomberg.com that allegedly contained defamatory statements regarding the ownership of shares in Velocity Capital Partner Berhad.
“The second investigation involves allegations of abuse of power and criminal intimidation against a member of the public identified as Albert Tei,” he said in a statement.
Kumar said both investigation papers would be referred to the Attorney General’s Chambers in the near future, in accordance with Article 145(3) of the Federal Constitution, for legal advice and further instructions.
“We advise the public not to speculate on the cases, as such actions could potentially affect or interfere with the investigation process,” he said.
“The police remain committed to ensuring transparency and integrity in their investigations and will not compromise when taking action against any individual found to have violated the law,” he added.

Title: Teacher pleads not guilty to seven sexual assault charges involving two students in Terengganu
Article:
SETIU, June 3 — A male teacher pleaded not guilty at the Setiu Sessions Court today to seven charges of physical sexual assault against two male students since last May.
Darusalam Ab Rahman, 42, made the plea after all the charges were read out before Judge Ahmad Fadhli Mahmud.
For the first three charges, the teacher was accused of communicating via the WhatsApp application with a 17-year-old male teenager with the intent to commit or facilitate physical sexual assault against the victim.
The offences were allegedly committed at a house in Kampung Anak Ikan, Besut, between 9:21 pm and 2.10 am on May 5, 8, and 10.
The charges were framed under Section 12(1) of the Sexual Offences Against Children Act (SOACA) 2017 and read together with Section 16(1) of the same Act, which provides for a prison sentence not exceeding five years and liability to whipping.
In addition, the accused faces two charges of committing physical sexual assault against the same teenager inside a car in front of an eatery in Kuala Terengganu at around 9 pm, and in front of a house in Kampung Anak Ikan at around 10 pm on May 11.
The charges were brought under Section 14(a) of SOACA 2017 and read together with Section 16(1) of the same Act, which carries a prison sentence not exceeding 20 years and liability to whipping.
Meanwhile, Darusalam was also slapped with two charges of committing physical sexual assault against a 15-year-old male teenager in a secondary school toilet in Besut at around 4pm on May 8, under Section 14(a) of SOACA 2017 and read together with Section 16(1) of the same Act, which carries a similar punishment.
Judge Ahmad Fadhli allowed the accused bail of RM22,000 for all charges with one surety, alongside additional conditions requiring him to report to the nearest police station once a month and restraining him from contacting prosecution witnesses.
The court fixed July 6 for mention and submission of documents at the Besut Sessions Court.
The prosecution was handled by Deputy Public Prosecutor Muhammad Akmal Mohd Jazlee, while the accused was represented by a lawyer (who declined to be named). — Bernama

Title: Tuanku Syed Sirajuddin wants Perlis MB given space to govern until end of assembly term
Article:
KANGAR, June 3 — The Raja of Perlis, Tuanku Syed Sirajuddin Jamalullail, wants Menteri Besar Abu Bakar Hamzah to be given the space to continue leading the state administration until the end of the current Perlis State Assembly term without any interference.
His Royal Highness said political stability is a crucial factor that will influence investors’ confidence to invest in the state.
As such, Tuanku Syed Sirajuddin called on all state assemblymen to set aside their egos and extend full and continuous cooperation to Abu Bakar for the benefit of the people and the state.
“I wish to remind all Honourable Members that this is my government. Therefore, I hope that the Menteri Besar will be given the space to continue leading until the end of the 15th Perlis State Legislative Assembly term without any interference that could disrupt the plans that have been put in place.
“All Honourable Members are reminded to prioritise the interests of the people over personal interests,” he said in his royal address at the opening of the fourth term of the 15th Perlis State Legislative Assembly sitting at Kompleks Seri Putra here today.
His royal address was delivered on his behalf by the Raja Muda of Perlis, Tuanku Syed Faizuddin Putra Jamalullail.
Tuanku Syed Sirajuddin said differences in political views should not become a barrier or obstacle to sincere efforts to bring development and investment into the state.
The Ruler also said that the state government must always maintain good relations with the federal government, particularly in terms of development and various forms of support for the benefit of the people of Perlis.
“I commend the series of visits and courtesy calls made by the Menteri Besar to various ministries to strengthen cooperation between the state and federal governments.
“I hope that these noble efforts will continue to be enhanced and sustained to ensure that the state’s development agenda can be implemented smoothly and effectively,” he said. — Bernama

Title: Lab assistant facing murder charge over Klang delivery rider’s death pleads not guilty to drug offence
Article:
KLANG, June 3 — A laboratory assistant who was previously charged with killing a motorcyclist in a road crash changed his plea to not guilty on one drug-related charge in the Magistrate’s Court here today.
R. Saktygaanapathy, 28, changed his plea after the amended charge was read out again before Magistrate Nor Azilah Mat.
According to the amended charge, he allegedly administered tetrahydrocannabinol (THC) into his own body at the Narcotics Criminal Investigation Division Office, South Klang District Police Headquarters (IPD) at 3.30 pm on March 29, 2026.
On April 1, he pleaded guilty to a charge of administering to himself the drugs benzo and THC at the same time and location.
The charge under Section 15(1)(a) of the Dangerous Drugs Act 1952 provides for a maximum fine of RM5,000 or imprisonment of up to two years, as well as supervision for no more than three years, if convicted.
His lawyer Aravind Raj told reporters outside the court that his client did not understand the implications and consequences of the guilty plea made previously because he was not represented by a lawyer when he was brought to court two months ago.
Nor Azilah set July 22 for case mention to obtain the chemical report.
The prosecution was conducted by Deputy Public Prosecutor Ahmad Sulfie Abu Hassan.
Meanwhile, in another court, Magistrate A. Karthiyayini also set July 22 for the mention of the murder case involving Saktygaanapathy to allow the prosecution to complete the post-mortem report.
Saktygaanapathy is charged with the murder of delivery rider Amirul Hafiz Omar, 33, on Jalan Raya Barat here at 11.47 am on March 29 while allegedly driving under the influence of drugs.
The charge is under Section 302 of the Penal Code, which carries the death penalty or imprisonment of between 30 and 40 years and, if not sentenced to death, may be subject to a minimum of 12 strokes of the cane, if convicted.
However, no plea was recorded from the accused regarding the murder case as it falls under the jurisdiction of the High Court.
Meanwhile, the lawyer representing the victim’s family, Mohd Faizi Che Abu, said they will file a claim for compensation exceeding RM1 million against the individual who caused Amirul Hafiz’s death, no later than next month.
According to him, they are completing related documents, including assessing the victim’s actual income to determine the appropriate amount of the claim. — Bernama

Title: Perlis ruler urges state to pursue high-impact investments, explore new revenue sources for sustainable growth
Article:
KANGAR, June 3 — The Perlis state government must explore innovative approaches suited to local potential to address increasingly complex economic challenges, said the Raja of Perlis, Tuanku Syed Sirajuddin Jamalullail.
Tuanku Syed Sirajuddin said this includes strengthening high-value sectors, attracting strategic investments, and leveraging geographical advantages as well as existing resources.
His Royal Highness said the approaches adopted should be based on best practices that have proven successful, whether domestically or internationally, while taking into account the state’s specific context.
“Focus should be given to high-impact initiatives capable of generating long-term returns, rather than short-term programmes that offer limited value to the state’s economy,” he said in his royal address at the opening of the fourth term of the Perlis State Legislative Assembly sitting at Kompleks Seri Putra here today.
Also in attendance was the Raja Perempuan of Perlis, Tuanku Tengku Fauziah Tengku Abdul Rashid.
The Raja Muda of Perlis, Tuanku Syed Faizuddin Putra Jamalullail, and the Raja Puan Muda of Perlis, Tuanku Dr Hajah Lailatul Shahreen Akashah Khalil, were also present.
Tuanku Syed Sirajuddin also emphasised the importance of efforts to generate state revenue and to explore new, more sustainable and resilient sources of income.
Meanwhile, His Royal Highness stressed that all expenditure must be carried out prudently, transparently and with integrity, while delivering maximum returns to the people in the implementation of development projects in Perlis.
“I am of the view that true progress is not measured solely by figures, but by the level of well-being genuinely experienced by all segments of society. Therefore, every policy and initiative must be carefully designed, with a focus on improving the quality of life of the people in a comprehensive, inclusive and sustainable manner.
Tuanku Syed Sirajuddin also expressed hope that the state government will continue to maintain good relations with the federal government to ensure the well-being of the state. — Bernama

Title: GOF foils bid to smuggle exotic birds from Thailand, seizes parrots worth RM100,000
Article:
KOTA BHARU, June 3 — Four exotic birds worth approximately RM100,000, believed to have been smuggled from Thailand, were seized by the General Operations Force (GOF) in an operation near the illegal Kak Nah jetty in Pengkalan Kubor, Tumpat, yesterday.
GOF Southeast Brigade Commander SAC Ahmad Radzi Hussain said the seizure was carried out under Op Taring Wawasan Kelantan at about 7 pm after a Strike Force team detected a man unloading several cages on the Malaysian side of Sungai Golok.
He said the suspect fled by boat towards the Thai riverbank upon realising the presence of GOF personnel.
“Inspection of the abandoned cages found two Blue-and-Gold Macaws and two Blue-fronted Amazon parrots, believed to have been brought in without valid permits.
“Also seized were two cages used to transport the birds, bringing the total value of the seizure to an estimated RM100,200,” he said in a statement today.
According to Ahmad Radzi, the case has been handed over to the Kelantan Department of Wildlife and National Parks (Perhilitan) for further investigation and action under the Wildlife Conservation Act 2010 (Act 716).
He said the exotic birds are believed to be intended for local buyers, adding that patrols and monitoring along the border will continue to be intensified to curb wildlife smuggling activities. — Bernama

Title: Perlis receives RM661.7m under 13MP as ruler calls for faster project implementation
Article:
KANGAR, June 3 — Perlis has received an allocation of RM661.70 million under the Rolling Plan 1 (RP1) of the 13th Malaysia Plan (13MP), with expenditure performance reaching RM87.46 million, or 13.22 per cent, as of last April.
The Raja of Perlis, Tuanku Syed Sirajuddin Jamalullail, said the spending involved the implementation of 354 projects.
“Under the Rolling Plan 5 (RP5) of the 12th Malaysia Plan (12MP), Perlis received an allocation of RM632.56 million involving 188 projects. Of that amount, RM474.17 million was spent, equivalent to 74.96 per cent last year,” he said.
His Royal Highness said this in his royal address at the opening of the fourth term of the Perlis State Legislative Assembly sitting at Kompleks Seri Putra here today.
His address was delivered on his behalf by the Raja Muda of Perlis, Tuanku Syed Faizuddin Putra Jamalullail.
Also in attendance were the Raja Perempuan of Perlis, Tuanku Tengku Fauziah Tengku Abdul Rashid, and the Raja Puan Muda of Perlis, Tuanku Dr Hajah Lailatul Shahreen Akashah Khalil.
Tuanku Syed Sirajuddin said that the implementation of all development projects must be expedited so that their benefits can be enjoyed by the people within a reasonable timeframe.
“I also call on all departments and agencies that have been allocated funds to take immediate steps to implement projects and ensure that spending is carried out efficiently and in an orderly manner,” he said.
The Ruler said this is crucial to stimulate the state’s economic activities, enabling the benefits of the economic cycle to be felt more quickly by the people through increased employment opportunities, income and local business activities.
His Royal Highness added that efficiency in implementation not only boosts public confidence but also ensures that the state’s financial resources are utilised optimally without wastage or undue delays. — Bernama

Title: Woman pleads guilty to culpable homicide over death of one of her newborn twins in Johor Bahru
Article:
JOHOR BAHRU, June 3 — A 22-year-old woman pleaded guilty at the Sessions Court here today to a charge of culpable homicide over the death of one of her newborn twin daughters last month.
The accused, Nuran Batrisya Jamaludin, nodded and pleaded guilty after the charge was read out to her before Judge Mohd Zamir Suhaimee.
According to the charge sheet, she was accused of causing the death of a baby girl with the intention of causing bodily injury likely to result in death.
The offence was allegedly committed in the car park of Blok Rajawali at the Program Perumahan Rakyat Melana Indah flats in Taman Sutera Utama here between 11am and 2.30pm on May 20.
Nuran Batrisya was charged under Section 304(a) of the Penal Code for culpable homicide not amounting to murder, which carries a prison sentence of up to 30 years and a fine upon conviction.
Earlier, Deputy Public Prosecutor R. Nevina did not offer bail.
However, the accused, who is unmarried and unemployed, applied for bail on the grounds that she is caring for the victim’s twin sibling and has family commitments.
Under Section 304(a) of the Penal Code, bail may be granted at the discretion of the Sessions Court or High Court.
The court granted bail of RM20,000 with two local sureties and imposed additional conditions. The accused was ordered to surrender her passport to the court, report to the nearest police station once a month and refrain from interfering with witnesses.
The court fixed July 3 for mention, submission of documents and sentencing.
It was previously reported that police arrested a young mother for allegedly throwing her newborn baby girl from the 10th floor of a residential flat here last month.
Acting Johor police chief Deputy Commissioner Hoo Chuan Huat said police received a report on May 20 at 2.40pm regarding the discovery of the baby’s body in the parking area of a residential flat.
Based on investigations, police believed the suspect, who is the baby’s biological mother, was responsible for the incident.
She was detained at the compound of Sultanah Aminah Hospital (HSA) before being arrested.

Title: Chinese national pleads not guilty to murdering compatriot at Cheras shopping mall restaurant
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 3 — A former restaurant waitress, a Chinese national, pleaded not guilty in the High Court here today to murdering her female fellow national by stabbing her multiple times at a restaurant in a shopping mall in Cheras two years ago.
Yang Yahong, 36, made the plea after the charge was read before Judge Datuk Aslam Zainuddin.
The woman was accused of murdering her colleague, cashier Zhang Wei, 34, at 4.30pm on November 29, 2024.
She was charged under Section 302 of the Penal Code, which provides for the death penalty or imprisonment of between 30 and 40 years, and is also liable to not less than 12 strokes of the cane upon conviction.
